acceptance_criteria: Identify the runner identity without committing account email, project identifier, token, or credential JSON.
Verify Vertex access with a minimal-spend preflight and no benchmark run.
Keep the selected next model endpoint as gemini-3.1-pro-preview-customtools only if the original endpoint has verified access.
Record the auth surface, endpoint, region, and spend ceiling for the next provider smoke.
Make no leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain claim.

evidence.notes: Ephemeral GCE VM attached to the dedicated Telos runner identity reached the original Vertex custom-tools endpoint with HTTP 200, one candidate, and usage metadata.
Sanitized serial-observed probe transcript records vm_created=true, the one-line Vertex probe result, and vm_deleted=true.
Cloud IAM side effects are limited to a dedicated runner short ID with Vertex user permission and operator attach permission; token-impersonation permission was removed.
Review confirms this is access recovery only and does not claim provider capability or benchmark performance.
No production or live-domain behavior changed. The ephemeral access-probe VM was deleted after the probe; the dedicated runner identity is retained for the next pre-registered smoke.

falsifiers: The gate must be blocked if the dedicated runner identity cannot reach the original Vertex model endpoint.
The gate must be blocked if the probe requires committing account email, project identifier, token, credential JSON, or raw secrets.
The gate must be blocked if the probe starts CodeClash, SWE-bench, a model sweep, or a leaderboard run.
The gate must be blocked if cloud side effects cannot be recorded and bounded.
The gate must not select a replacement model inside this result.
stated_goal: Recover a usable Vertex access path for the already selected gemini-3.1-pro-preview-customtools provider smoke without changing the model, running CodeClash, or committing sensitive Google Cloud identifiers.
